seoTitle: How to Download Microsoft Excel for Free (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn the legitimate ways to get Microsoft Excel for free in 2026, from the web app and mobile version to student and corporate access paths.
intro: If you need spreadsheet power without paying for a license, Microsoft actually offers several legal, no-cost routes into Excel. This guide walks through the browser-based version, the mobile app, and institutional access paths so you can pick the one that fits your situation and start working right away.

steps.heading: Sign up for a free Microsoft account
steps.body: Head to the official Microsoft 365 site and create a free account using any personal email address. This unlocks the web-based version of Excel at no cost.

steps.heading: Open Excel from the web dashboard
steps.body: Once signed in, find Excel in your browser dashboard and open a blank workbook to start editing immediately, with no download required.

steps.heading: Try the mobile app route
steps.body: On a phone or tablet, install the free Excel or Microsoft 365 app from your app store, log in with the same free account, and edit spreadsheets on the go.

steps.heading: Check for student or corporate access
steps.body: If you have a school or work email address, you may qualify for a free full desktop license through your institution's Microsoft 365 program.

steps.heading: Sync and open local files
steps.body: Use OneDrive to store your spreadsheets in the cloud, and the upload option in the web app to open existing local .xlsx files.

faq.question: Is the free version of Excel really free forever?
faq.answer: Yes, the web-based version tied to a free Microsoft account has no time limit, though it has fewer advanced features than the paid desktop app.

faq.question: Do I need to enter payment details to sign up?
faq.answer: No, creating a basic free Microsoft account only requires an email address and does not require a credit card.

faq.question: Can I open files created in the desktop version?
faq.answer: Yes, you can upload and edit standard .xlsx files inside the free web version, though some advanced formatting may differ slightly.

faq.question: Is there a safe way to get the full desktop app for free?
faq.answer: The safest free path to the full desktop app is through a valid student or employer Microsoft 365 subscription, if your school or company provides one.
